# 1099Deductions — Full Tax Reference 2026
> Complete IRS-verified tax deduction data for 1099 independent contractors. All figures current for 2026 tax year.

## Key Tax Numbers 2026

| Parameter | Value |
|---|---|
| IRS mileage rate 2026 | 72.5¢/mile |
| SE tax rate | 15.3% (12.4% SS + 2.9% Medicare) |
| SE tax formula | net × 0.9235 × 0.153 |
| 1099-NEC threshold (OBBBA 2026) | $2,000 |
| No Tax on Tips cap | $25,000 |
| QBI deduction | 20% of net profit |
| Standard deduction (single) | $15,000 |
| Standard deduction (married) | $30,000 |
| Quarterly deadlines | Apr 15, Jun 16, Sep 15, Jan 15 |

## Platform Tax Estimates 2026

| Platform | Annual Net | SE Tax | Federal Total | Quarterly |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| DoorDash / Amazon Flex | $40,000 | $5,652 | $8,852 | $2,213 |
| Uber / Instacart / Lyft | $35,000 | $4,944 | $7,750 | $1,938 |
| Airbnb / Turo / OnlyFans | $50,000 | $7,065 | $11,565 | $2,891 |
| Etsy / Shipt | $30,000 | $4,239 | $6,448 | $1,612 |
| Rover | $25,000 | $3,532 | $5,340 | $1,335 |
| TaskRabbit | $45,000 | $6,357 | $10,160 | $2,540 |
| Upwork / Fiverr | $60,000 | $8,478 | $14,278 | $3,570 |

## Top Deductions with Amounts

| Deduction | Max / Typical | IRS Form |
|---|---|---|
| Mileage (72.5¢/mile) | $14,500 at 20k miles | Schedule C |
| Home office ($5/sqft) | $1,500 (300 sqft) | Form 8829 |
| Health insurance | 100% of premiums | Schedule 1 |
| SEP-IRA contribution | up to $69,000 | Schedule 1 |
| Solo 401k | up to $69,000 | Schedule 1 |
| Phone/internet (business %) | $600–$1,200 | Schedule C |
| Equipment / supplies | actual cost | Schedule C |
| Professional services | actual cost | Schedule C |
| Half of SE tax | ~$2,826 on $40k | Schedule 1 |
| QBI deduction | 20% net profit | Form 8995 |

## Mileage Examples

| Miles/year | Deduction |
|---|---|
| 10,000 | $7,250 |
| 20,000 | $14,500 |
| 30,000 | $21,750 |
| 40,000 | $29,000 |

## OBBBA 2026 Key Changes
- 1099-NEC filing threshold raised from $600 to $2,000
- No Tax on Tips: up to $25,000 tips excluded from federal income tax
- QBI deduction made permanent (was expiring)
- Standard deduction increased to $15,000 single / $30,000 married

Source: IRS.gov, OBBBA 2026 legislation
